items.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
items.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations demanding an immediate ceasefire while Russia insists its strikes target only military infrastructure.

items.title: Global temperatures set to stay near record levels: UN weather agency
items.description: The world is heading into another period of dangerous heat, a new UN report warns on Thursday, and it’s nearly certain global temperatures over the next five years will stay “at or near record levels” as climate change accelerates across land and sea.

items.title: WHO chief calls for ceasefire amid DR Congo Ebola outbreak
items.description: The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) headed to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) on Thursday as the country continues to combat a deadly resurgence of Ebola in its volatile eastern region where instability is rife.

items.title: UN80 Initiative: ‘Critical new phase’ for UN reform effort
items.description: UN chief António Guterres has outlined further progress in the wide-ranging UN80 Initiative, a major reform and restructuring project aimed at making the global body more effective, agile and better equipped to respond to global challenges.

items.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
items.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.

items.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
items.description: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.

items.title: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza
items.description: Intensified Israeli airstrikes overnight in Lebanon forced people to again flee their homes, while humanitarians in the Gaza Strip report continued restrictions in bringing aid into the enclave, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

items.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief
items.description: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.

items.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
items.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

items.title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
items.description: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
items.description: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confidence in the Security Council’s ability to respond to international crises.

items.title: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
items.description: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus, said on Monday.

items.title: Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions
items.description: With the World Cup just days away, 16 greats of the game have been appointed Football for the Goals (FFTG) Champions, using their profiles to raise awareness of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and advocate for a better future for people and the planet.

items.title: Displaced in Gaza: From home comforts to the shattering horror of war
items.description: In front of a simple tent in Gaza that offers no protection from the cold or the heat, in a crowded camp where tents lack privacy and basic services, Umm Ahmad sat down to speak to UN News about her life in Gaza before the war and what it has become now.

items.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
items.description: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.

items.title: WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness
items.description: The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.

items.title: Review of landmark nuclear treaty breaks up without consensus, raising arms race fears
items.description: After four weeks of negotiations at UN Headquarters in New York, the 11th Review Conference of the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) ended on Friday without consensus on a final declaration.

items.title: From Kharkiv to the UK’s health service: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her life – and helps others do the same
items.description: On 23 February 2022, in her home city of Kharkiv, Dr Inna Soldatenko finished work, collected her daughter from school, cooked dinner and prepared a lecture for her students. The next morning, she awoke to explosions.

items.title: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
items.description: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.

items.title: World Urban Forum backs ‘Baku Call to Action’ on global housing crisis
items.description: A landmark gathering of more than 57,000 participants – the largest in the history of the World Urban Forum – closed on Friday in Baku with an urgent call to rethink how the world houses its people, as a new roadmap urges governments, cities and communities to act collectively on a crisis affecting billions.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
items.description: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had struck a Russian military drone command headquarters, according to news reports. Follow live coverage below and for full meetings coverage of the council, click here.

items.title: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
items.description: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

items.title: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground
items.description: Dire conditions in Gaza marked by continuing violence, rodent infestations and the spread of infectious disease are being made worse by blockages of essential medical supplies, UN agencies warned on Friday.

items.title: Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court
items.description: The UN International Court of Justice (ICJ) on Thursday ruled that the right to strike is protected under a core International Labour Organization (ILO) convention, in a landmark advisory opinion settling a long-running dispute between workers and employers worldwide.

items.title: Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment
items.description: As gangs continue to “terrorise” communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.

items.title: Gaza risks ‘permanent’ state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears
items.description: As Gaza’s fragile ceasefire frays and humanitarian conditions deteriorate, a senior UN envoy warned the Security Council on Thursday that delays in implementing the Council-backed transition plan for the enclave will only increase suffering and undermine recovery.

items.title: UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo
items.description: United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support.

items.title: War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls
items.description: Thirteen-year-old Mona still remembers the moment the second airstrike hit.

items.title: Building the smart city: Promise, pitfalls and the people at its heart
items.description: From AI-powered transit systems to digital twins and flood-proof parks, cities are embracing technology at an unprecedented scale. But as innovation accelerates, experts warn that inclusion, trust and security will determine who truly benefits.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace
items.description: The Security Council is discussing the stalled progress in Gaza as the fragile ceasefire there limps on, and deteriorating conditions across the West Bank amid continued civilian casualties and mounting humanitarian needs. Discussions are likely to focus on who governs Gaza, and how - with no disarmament in sight, together with efforts to support recovery.

items.title: 《北部湾经济区开放发展规划》公开征求意见：构建平陆运河综合交通体系
items.description: 近日，《北部湾经济区开放发展规划(征求意见稿)》公开征求意见。其中提到，拟推进重大基础设施建设。构建平陆运河综合交通体系，优化北部湾经济区民航机场一体化规划布局和支线建设，加快推进北海机场改扩建前期工作，推动南宁机场军民航分离，打造面向东盟的区域航空枢纽。推动中国—东盟信息港升级发展，强化跨境数字基础设施互联互通，合理有序推进算力中心布局，打造面向东盟的算力中心。坚持风光水核多能并举、源网荷储协同发展，加快建设北部湾海上风电基地。加快推进环北部湾水资源配置工程、平陆运河、邕北灌区、平陆灌区等重大水利水运基础设施建设。

items.title: 老挝人革党中央总书记、国家主席通伦将访华
items.description: 中新社北京5月29日电 中共中央对外联络部发言人胡兆明5月29日宣布：应中共中央总书记、国家主席习近平邀请，老挝人革党中央总书记、国家主席通伦将于6月2日至6日对中国进行国事访问。(完)

items.title: 暑期入境游订单涨超两成 外国游客解锁中国旅行新方式
items.description: 暑期入境游订单涨超两成

items.title: 中国气象局：今年春夏季厄尔尼诺处于快速发展期
items.description:
5月29日，中国气象局举行6月新闻发布会。邵萌 摄

　　中新网5月29日电(记者 邵萌)5月29日，中国气象局举行新闻发布会。记者从会上获悉，5月赤道中东太平洋海温进入厄尔尼诺状态，夏秋季将发展成为一次中等及以上强度的厄尔尼诺事件，于秋冬季达到峰值，发生强厄尔尼诺事件的概率正在增大，预计明年春季衰减。#8194;今年春夏季厄尔尼诺处于快速发展期，大气对海温演变有显著的响应，有利于西北太平洋副热带高压明显增强，引导更多的水汽输送到我国陆地区域。历史统计表明，厄尔尼诺发展背景下，易出现我国长江以南地区夏秋季降水较常年偏多、全国大部气温偏高的情况。

items.title: 中国气象局：6月这些地方需防范高温热浪天气
items.description:
5月29日，中国气象局举行6月新闻发布会。邵萌 摄

　　中新网5月29日电(记者 邵萌)5月29日，中国气象局举行新闻发布会。记者从会上获悉，5月，全国平均气温16.9℃，较常年同期偏高0.6℃。上海平均气温为历史同期最高，吉林为第二高。预计6月，华北中南部至黄淮等地出现阶段性高温热浪风险较高。建议加强对高温预警的关注，防范其对旅游出行、生产施工、人群健康、能源保供等产生的不利影响，提前采取防暑降温、科学调度等措施。

items.title: 旅游业应跳出“走马观花”模式
items.description: 据文化和旅游部数据中心测算，今年一季度，全国乡村旅游接待总人次为7.93亿，同比增长12.2%。中国社会科学院旅游研究中心此前发布的《旅游绿皮书：2025～2026年中国旅游发展分析与预测》指出，2025年以来，文旅融合、数智赋能、赛事经济、入境旅游成为释放旅游发展潜力的重要支点。随着游客群体代际更迭和消费心理变化，旅游已升级为融合文化认知、情感共鸣与价值认同的复合型体验活动。

items.title: 中欧班列（长沙）跨境电商专列首发
items.description: 中新社长沙5月29日电(张雪盈 陈志群)首列中欧班列(长沙)跨境电商专列29日从湖南长沙启程，将经由霍尔果斯口岸一路西行至波兰马拉舍维奇。

items.title: 中国气象局：今年5月全国平均降水量为近10年同期最多
items.description: 中新网5月29日电(记者 邵萌)5月29日，中国气象局举行新闻发布会，记者从会上获悉，5月全国平均降水量为近10年同期最多，暴雨过程极端性突出，强降水持续时间长、影响范围广。有3次区域暴雨过程影响我国。5月13日至21日的暴雨过程是今年以来也是5月历史(1961年以来)最强区域暴雨过程，具有显著的“持续时间长、影响范围广、极端性强”等特征，最大影响面积位居5月历史第一位，持续时间并列5月历史第一；此次过程强降雨高度集中在广东、广西、湖北、湖南、贵州等地，有68个国家级气象站日降水量突破春季极值。5月24日起，新一轮区域性暴雨过程开始影响我国，强降雨主要分布在湖北、江苏等地，有14个国家级气象站日降水量突破春季极值。

items.title: 美国蓝色起源公司火箭在发动机点火测试中发生爆炸
items.description: 中新网5月29日电 据美联社报道，当地时间5月28日晚，美国蓝色起源公司的“新格伦”火箭在发动机点火测试中发生爆炸。事件未造成人员受伤。

items.title: 从各省份“十五五”规划纲要看全国发展一盘棋
items.description: 原标题：协力同心启新篇 步调一致向前进——从各省份“十五五”规划纲要看全国发展一盘棋

items.title: 第10届中国—南亚博览会将于6月11日至16日在昆明举行
items.description: 中新网5月29日电 国新办29日就中国与南亚经贸合作及第10届中国—南亚博览会有关情况举行新闻发布会。商务部副部长鄢东在会上表示，第10届中国—南亚博览会将于6月11日至16日在昆明举行。

items.title: 四进四送润民心 太极惠民进社区——民进四平市委会开展传统文化公益服务活动
items.description: 为深入落实民进“四进四送”社会服务工作要求，按照市委统战部“汇智同心、党派同行”社会服务活动方案工作部署，5月27日，民进四平市委会走进铁东区北门街道泰安社区，开展太极拳文化宣讲与公益展演活动，将优质传统文化服务送到群众家门口。民进四平市委会专职副主委胡冬梅、秘书长姜楠出席活动。

items.title: 香港特区政府官员：冀港合作可双向赋能
items.description: 中新社河北雄安5月29日电 (王夏菲  赵安源  韩冰)“雄安新区发展速度非常快，很多经验值得香港学习。”香港特别行政区政府投资推广署助理署长刘智元28日在河北雄安新区接受中新社采访时表示，香港将深化冀港在金融、科创等领域的全方位合作，尤其将围绕算力、人工智能等新兴赛道，推动两地资源双向对接、优势互补，实现互利共赢。

items.title: 5月进入厄尔尼诺状态 我国气温较常年偏高南方降水多
items.description: 记者29日从国家气候中心了解到，5月赤道中东太平洋海温进入厄尔尼诺状态，夏秋季发展成为一次中等及以上强度的厄尔尼诺事件，于秋冬季达到峰值，发生强厄尔尼诺事件的概率正在增大，预计明年春季衰减。

items.title: 以军扩大对黎巴嫩和加沙“双线”军事行动
items.description: 中新社北京5月29日电  综合消息：以色列近期对加沙地带和黎巴嫩的“双线”军事行动呈升级态势。黎以军方代表团定于29日在美国五角大楼举行会谈。以总理内塔尼亚胡28日称，已下令要求以军控制加沙地带70%区域。

items.title: 暴雨蓝色预警：8省区部分地区有大到暴雨 云南四川等地有大暴雨
items.description: 中新网5月29日电 据中央气象台网站消息，中央气象台5月29日10时继续发布暴雨蓝色预警：预计，5月29日14时至30日14时，云南中东部、四川西南部、广西西南部和北部、广东北部和东部、福建北部和南部、湖南南部、贵州南部、江西南部等地部分地区有大到暴雨，其中，云南北部和东南部、四川西南部等地部分地区有大暴雨(100～180毫米)。上述部分地区伴有短时强降水(最大小时降雨量20～50毫米，局地可超过60毫米)，局地有雷暴大风或冰雹等强对流天气。

items.title: 裁缝铺变身“网红自习室” 让传统手艺“活”在当下
items.description: “老匠人+新青年”组合碰撞出别样火花

items.title: 中国驻菲律宾大使馆：64名被菲方羁押的中国公民获释
items.description: 中新网5月29日电 据“中国驻菲律宾大使馆”微信公众号29日消息，5月28日晚，64名被菲方羁押的中国公民获释，中国驻菲律宾使馆派员赴现场照料。还有6人正在办理获释手续。他们此前在东米萨米斯省一钢铁厂工作，5月15日遭菲执法部门查扣羁押。菲司法部日前作出裁定：对涉事中国公民涉嫌违反菲核能安全法、移民法、劳工法等指控证据不足，应予释放。

items.title: 商务部加大货源组织力度 全国生活必需品市场“运行稳+供应足”
items.description: 央视网消息：5月28日，商务部举行例行新闻发布会，商务部新闻发言人介绍，近期我国南方多地经历了今年汛期以来最强的降雨过程。商务部加大生活必需品货源的组织力度，目前，全国生活必需品市场运行平稳、供应充足。

items.title: 打造七条航道吸引青年创新创业 新业态让海岛活力拉满
items.description: 温州洞头区由300多个零碎小岛组成，由于地处海岛交通末梢，发展一直落后于周边地区。如今，全国各地不少年轻人来到岛上创业，原本寂静的海岛充满了青春活力。来看记者的调研。

items.title: “假煽情”还是“真必要”为啥部分家长反对成人礼？
items.description: 临近毕业季，全国不少高中都在为高三学生举办成人礼。身穿礼服、挽着父母的手臂走过“成人门”、互换感恩信与礼物、相拥而泣……这些环节似乎已成为标配，也成为许多学子难忘的记忆。

items.title: 前4个月我国交通运输经济运行平稳
items.description: 央视网消息(新闻联播)：交通运输部最新数据显示，1—4月，我国完成营业性货运量181.7亿吨，同比增长3.6%。

items.title: 中国驻福冈总领事馆提醒领区中国公民注意防范第6号强台风
items.description: 中新网5月29日电 据“中国驻福冈总领事馆”微信公众号29日消息，据日本气象厅预报，今年第6号台风预计将于6月1日至2日期间伴随暴风区以强台风级别直击冲绳县，冲绳县和鹿儿岛县奄美地区将出现狂风、暴雨、巨浪。同时，台风可能引发河流泛滥和泥石流、建筑物倒塌等次生灾害，上述地区航班、铁路、公路、轮渡等公共交通或将因恶劣天气受到不同程度延误或取消。

items.title: 主播说联播丨2030年的城市什么样？重磅文件绘出蓝图
items.description: 我们生活的城市，未来五年将如何更新？国务院日前印发《城市更新“十五五”规划》，为城市更新提供了“任务书”和“路线图”。

items.title: 正值华南“龙舟水” 专家提醒这些地区需警惕洪水灾害
items.description: 当前，我国南方持续降雨，华南地区进入了“龙舟水”关键时期。什么是“龙舟水”？今年我国“龙舟水”有什么特点？如何防范可能带来的洪涝灾害？

items.title: 焦点访谈丨战阴雨 抢农时 湖北、河南、山东三大小麦主产区这样保丰收
items.description: 夺取夏粮丰收，事关全年粮食大局，是稳住粮食安全基本盘的关键一仗。近期的持续降雨，对湖北、河南、山东三大小麦主产区造成不同程度影响，给小麦的抢收、烘干、田间管护、农机服务等等带来多重挑战。这三个地方的小麦生长阶段各不相同，应对阴雨天气的举措也不尽相同。农时紧、任务重，一起去看这些地方如何全力以赴护航夏粮丰产丰收。

items.title: 前4个月全国网上商品和服务零售额增长6.6%
items.description: 央视网消息(新闻联播)：记者从商务部了解到，今年前四个月，全国网上商品和服务零售额同比增长6.6%，网上商品零售额对社会消费品零售总额增长贡献率为72.2%。

items.title: 勇立潮头大湾区丨突破1000万辆次！港澳居民“一脚油门”畅行湾区
日本学者：政府应重新审视和平宪法意义
items.description: 近来，日本政府在情报领域动作频频，推动多层面整合升级情报力量。

items.title: 南航6月2日起复航广州-昭通直飞航线
items.description: 中新网广州5月29日电 (记者 郭军)记者29日从中国南方航空(以下简称南航)获悉，南航计划于6月2日起正式复航广州至云南昭通直飞往返客运航线。该航线的复航将极大缩短两地间的时空距离，结束了两地无直飞航班的现状，架起了云南东北部至粤港澳大湾区的“空中通道”。
